description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by four prominent black metal hoops. The wood has a weathered, natural finish, showing visible grain patterns and knots, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, tightly fitted within the metal bands. The metal hoops are evenly spaced and provide structural reinforcement, ensuring the barrel maintains its shape under load. There are no visible handles, spouts, or markings on the exterior, and the surface appears slightly rough but well-crafted. This type of barrel is commonly used for storage or transport of liquids or dry goods in urban, industrial, or outdoor settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with black metal hoops, featuring a rustic, sturdy appearance and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel's front shows the wood grain and all four black metal hoops clearly, with the top surface fully visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the curvature of the staves and the even spacing of the metal hoops, with subtle wood texture details.
description_view_2: The barrel's opposite side is shown, with similar wood grain and metal hoop arrangement, and no additional features visible.
description_view_3: This view displays the rear side, again showing the consistent wood texture and metal banding, with no handles or markings.
